name: La Fogata's Green Chicken Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Cilantro Leaves, loosely packed, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil tomatillos in water with garlic, chiles, salt, and pepper until soft, about 15-20 minutes.
2. Puree the cooked sauce in a blender until liquefied. While blending, add washed cilantro leaves.
3. Set aside. The sauce yields about 2 and 1/2 cups and will thicken upon standing. You may need to thin with chicken stock.
4. Simmer chicken in lightly salted water until tender, about 10-15 minutes. Cool chicken; it will be slightly undercooked.
5. Shred the cooked chicken and then, just prior to serving, heat in 1 cup of chicken stock. This will heat the chicken without overcooking it.
6. In a medium skillet, heat oil to 300 degrees.
7. Pass tortillas into hot oil for a few seconds to soften and seal. Remove carefully and set aside between paper towels.
8. Assemble by filling softened tortillas with shredded chicken and 1-2 tablespoons of sauce.
9. Roll up and place seam-side-down in the casserole.
10. Pour green sauce over top and garnish with sour cream and cheese.
11. Place in a 375-degree oven for 5-8 minutes, or just long enough to melt the cheese. Note: Sauce may be made ahead of time, but the dish is best when chicken is freshly prepared.
